summary: Moved the PassiveWall action out of raw assembly in `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s` into a new `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_PassiveWall.c`/`.h` pair, with companion sdata2 literals in `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_PassiveWall.s`. The file report shows the new C file at 99.47% overall: six functions fully matched and `ftCo_800C1E64` still partial at 98.08%. The PR also added the PassiveWall motion-var layout, refined related function prototypes and common-data fields, and wired the new object files into `obj_files.mk`.
what_changed: Added `ftCo_PassiveWall.c` with C implementations or partial C/ASM wrappers for `ftCo_800C1D38`, `ftCo_800C1E0C`, `ftCo_800C1E64`, `ftCo_PassiveWall_Anim`, `ftCo_PassiveWall_IASA`, `ftCo_PassiveWall_Phys`, and `ftCo_PassiveWall_Coll`.
Deleted about 477 lines of PassiveWall-related text assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s`.

Added a small `ftCo_PassiveWall.s` containing sdata2 literals `ftCo_804D8C60`, `ftCo_804D8C64`, `ftCo_804D8C68`, `ftCo_804D8C70`, and `ftCo_804D8C78`, including `U32_TO_F32` and `S32_TO_F32` double constants.
Added `ftCo_PassiveWall.h` and moved PassiveWall prototypes out of the broad `ft_0C08.h` header.
Added `mv.co.passivewall` motion variables in `ftCommon/types.h`: `timer`, `x4`, `x8`, and `vel_y_exponent` at offsets `fp+2340` through `fp+234C`.
Refined `ftCommonData` around offsets `+760`, `+764`, and `+778`, naming `+778` as `passive_wall_vel_y_base` and exposing `x760`/`x764` used by PassiveWall setup/collision.
Changed `ftCo_DatAttrs.x100` from `int` to `float`, matching its use as PassiveWall horizontal velocity in `ftCo_PassiveWall_Anim`.

Tightened related prototypes: `ft_80083318` now takes a fighter gobj, callback, and event; `ftCo_800BFFD0` now takes `enum_t` and `bool`; `ftCo_800986B0` is exposed as `bool ftCo_800986B0(ftCo_GObj* gobj)`.
Added local includes of `ftCo_PassiveWall.h` in related common fighter files such as Damage, DownDamage, FlyReflect, Init, and wall-jump code.
Registered both the new C object and literal assembly object in `obj_files.mk`.
smart_moves: Split the decompilation by action module instead of leaving the code in the catch-all `ft_0C08.s`, making PassiveWall easier to search, type, and iterate on independently.
Kept hard-to-express literals in a tiny dedicated `.s` file, preserving sdata2 symbol placement and float/double conversion constants while still decompiling the behavior into C.
Used the action-specific motion-variable union member `mv.co.passivewall` to replace raw offsets `0x2340`, `0x2344`, `0x2348`, and `0x234C` with meaningful local state.
Modeled the left/right wall branch through collision flags and ECB corrected positions, reusing `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B11`/`MPCOLL_FLAGS_B05` and named collision structs rather than raw bit tests everywhere.
Isolated a small inline helper for the PassiveWall-to-PassiveWallJump transition, which helped express a repeated motion-state change and timer reset cleanly.
Preserved matching-sensitive details in the C source with `ASM` wrappers, `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` fallbacks, `#pragma peephole on`, and explicit unused-stack padding notes.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling a fighter action, move its callbacks into an action-named `ftCo_*` file and create a matching header, then remove only those prototypes from broader legacy headers.
For motion-state local storage, add a named member to `union ftCommon_MotionVars` once offsets are understood; this makes timer/state use clearer and reduces raw `fp+2340` style reasoning.
Common data fields should be named incrementally from observed usage. Here, `passive_wall_vel_y_base` was justified by its use in `powf` scaling of PassiveWall vertical velocity.

Type corrections in shared headers can be required for a match: `ftCo_DatAttrs.x100` becoming `float` aligns with multiplication into `self_vel.x`, and callback prototypes becoming concrete reduced `UNK` use.
Partial matches can still be valuable when most callbacks are fully matched and the remaining difficult function is isolated behind an `ASM`/C conditional.
Keep float constants and integer-to-double conversion helpers as named sdata2 symbols when the compiler will not naturally place or align them correctly.
naming_conventions: Action module files use `ftCo_PassiveWall.c` and `ftCo_PassiveWall.h` naming under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/`.
Action callbacks use the pattern `ftCo_PassiveWall_Anim`, `ftCo_PassiveWall_IASA`, `ftCo_PassiveWall_Phys`, and `ftCo_PassiveWall_Coll`.
Unknown but address-stable helpers retain address-style names such as `ftCo_800C1D38`, `ftCo_800C1E0C`, and `ftCo_800C1E64`.
Motion variables are grouped under `fp->mv.co.passivewall`, with partially understood fields left as `x4`/`x8` and known roles named `timer` and `vel_y_exponent`.
Common-data unknowns remain offset-like (`x760`, `x764`) unless usage supports a semantic name, as with `passive_wall_vel_y_base`.
Header include guards in this area use address-style names such as `GALE01_0C1D38`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the decompiled functions from `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s` but retained sdata2 literals in a new action-local assembly file.
Used `.balign 8` and explicit `.float`/`.8byte` directives for constants `-1`, `1`, `0`, `U32_TO_F32`, and `S32_TO_F32`.
Declared C-side extern literals for `ftCo_804D8C60`, `ftCo_804D8C64`, `ftCo_804D8C68`, `ftCo_804D8C70`, and `ftCo_804D8C78`.
Used conditional `ASM` functions with C bodies under non-`MUST_MATCH`/`WIP` and inline assembly fallbacks for matching-sensitive functions.
Included explicit unused stack padding comments/arrays in functions where stack layout affected matching.
Used `powf(p_ftCommonData->passive_wall_vel_y_base, fp->mv.co.passivewall.vel_y_exponent)` to model the signed-int-to-float conversion path tied to the `S32_TO_F32` literal.

File report evidence: `ftCo_PassiveWall_Anim`, `ftCo_PassiveWall_IASA`, `ftCo_800C1D38`, `ftCo_PassiveWall_Phys`, `ftCo_800C1E0C`, and `ftCo_PassiveWall_Coll` were reported as 100%, while `ftCo_800C1E64` remained 98.08%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
There is no direct reviewer rationale in the evidence; conclusions are based on the title, file report, changed files, and diff.
follow_up_queries: Investigate the remaining mismatch in `ftCo_800C1E64`, especially stack layout around the unused stack area and effect spawn position calculation.
Rename `ftCommonData.x760` and `ftCommonData.x764` once their PassiveWall timer/collision semantics are confirmed.
Determine semantic names for `mv.co.passivewall.x4` and `mv.co.passivewall.x8`; `x8` appears to gate a delayed transition to `ftCo_MS_PassiveWallJump`.
Validate whether `ftCo_DatAttrs.x100` should receive a semantic name related to PassiveWall horizontal velocity.
Check if the sdata2 literals can eventually be emitted from C without the dedicated `ftCo_PassiveWall.s` file.
